title: Fragrant Citrusy Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring chicken cooked in a flavorful citrusy sauce, perfect for special occasions. This recipe is macrobiotic-friendly and uses all-purpose seasoning.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, stirring occasionally.
Add the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the fresh lemon juice and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve the chicken and sauce over couscous or rice, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
